content: Problem Statements For TechSprint : PS1: The "Skill-Twin" Engine Core Issue: The "Degree Factory" syndrome—students graduate with high grades but low employability because the university curriculum lags behind industry trends. The Challenge: Create a Dynamic Curriculum Gap Analyzer. The software should scrape live job descriptions from platforms like LinkedIn/Naukri.com for specific roles (e.g., “Full Stack Dev”, “ML Developer”) and compare them against the university's current syllabus and the student's GitHub/Project portfolio along with CGPA. It should then generate a personalized "Bridge Course" roadmap for the student, suggesting specific micro-skills they are missing. SDG Alignment: SDG 4 (Quality Education) & SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth). GIET University, Gunupur PS2: The "Digital Archaeology" Challenge Core Issue: University websites are often "PDF graveyards." Critical notices (exam schedules, scholarship deadlines, bus routes) are uploaded as non-searchable images or scanned PDFs. Finding specific information requires clicking through dozens of links, leading to missed deadlines and confusion. The Challenge: Build a Semantic Search & Notification Engine that sits on top of the existing website. The system should periodically scrape the "Notices" section, use OCR (Optical Character Recognition) to read text inside scanned PDFs/Images, and index it into a vector database. The result? A "Perplexity-style" search bar where a student can ask: "What is the syllabus for the 3rd-semester Java lab?" or "Show me all scholarship notices for OBC students released in the last month," and get a direct answer, not a link to a file.SDG Alignment: SDG 4 (Quality Education) – Ensuring equitable access to information. PS3 : The Challenge: The "Bandwidth-Agnostic" Challenge Core Issue: University portals are becoming increasingly "heavy"—loaded with high-res banners, unoptimized scripts, and complex animations. For students in rural India or remote campus areas (Tier-3/4 regions) relying on spotty 2G/3G mobile data, accessing basic services like checking results, paying fees, or viewing attendance is impossible. The site times out before it loads, deepening the digital divide. The Challenge: Develop a "Bandwidth-Agnostic" Middleware or PWA (Progressive Web App) Wrapper for the university portal. This tool must act as a compression tunnel between the student and the university server. The system should intercept the university's heavy HTTP responses and: Strip the Fat: Automatically remove all non-essential media (images, videos, heavy fonts) and tracking scripts. Transcode on the Fly: Convert complex HTML structures into a pure "Text-Only" or "DataLite" interface that consumes less than 50KB per page load. Prevent "White Screens": Ensure the interface renders instantly, even if the backend data takes 20 seconds to fetch. SDG Alignment: SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities) – Ensuring students in remote geographies have equal access to institutional resources. PS 4: The “Holistic Interview Intelligence” Challenge Core Issue: Current interview preparation platforms are largely one-dimensional. While most systems focus only on what a candidate says, real-world interviews evaluate how a candidate communicates—including clarity of speech, tone, confidence, body language, and overall presence. As a result, candidates are often underprepared for actual interview scenarios. The Challenge: Build an AI-powered Interview Preparation & Evaluation Platform that goes beyond content analysis. The system should simulate real interview scenarios and analyze both verbal and non-verbal communication. Using audio and video inputs, the platform should evaluate speech clarity, confidence level, tone modulation, facial expressions, eye contact, and posture, and then provide actionable feedback to the candidate. The result? A smart interview coach where a student can practice mock interviews and receive insights like: • “Your answer was correct, but confidence was low.”
summary: The software should scrape live job descriptions from platforms like LinkedIn/Naukri.com for specific roles (e.g., “Full Stack Dev”, “ML Developer”) and compare them against the university's current syllabus and the student's GitHub/Project portfolio along with CGPA. For students in rural India or remote campus areas (Tier-3/4 regions) relying on spotty 2G/3G mobile data, accessing basic services like checking results, paying fees, or viewing attendance is impossible. Using audio and video inputs, the platform should evaluate speech clarity, confidence level, tone modulation, facial expressions, eye contact, and posture, and then provide actionable feedback to the candidate.
